forecasts.reasoning: The state of emergency (SOE) in Samoa was declared on March 31, 2025, in response to a severe energy crisis, and is officially scheduled to expire on April 29, 2025. The core uncertainty is whether the government will extend this SOE beyond its original 30-day mandate to cover April 30. Recent reporting as of April 21, 2025, indicates that the Electric Power Corporation (EPC) has officially cancelled its power rationing program. This was made possible by the arrival of temporary leased generators from New Zealand, which have successfully restored sufficient generation capacity to the grid. With the acute phase of the power shortage over and rationing cancelled, the primary political and practical justifications for maintaining emergency powers are effectively moot. Furthermore, while Samoa has recently faced a dengue fever outbreak, historical precedent and current reporting show this is being managed via a standard public health "outbreak" declaration rather than a nationwide SOE. Therefore, it is highly likely the current SOE will be allowed to lapse on April 29 as scheduled, leaving no SOE in effect on April 30.

Based on information available before the knowledge cutoff of April 21, 2025, Democratic Rep. Haley Stevens formally announced her candidacy for the U.S. Senate in Michigan. She made the announcement on her X (formerly Twitter) account (@Haleyformi), stating, "It's official, Michigan. I'm running to be your next United States senator." This was also reported by The Washington Post on April 21, 2025. Since the announcement occurred before May 1, 2025, the criteria for a YES resolution have undeniably been met. The probability is capped at 0.95 in accordance with the instructions.
